1.errorText: A woman phones Dreamtime Travel about the Whale Watch Experience. The tour lasts three days and the transport is by coach. They take up to fifty people, but usually run it with just twelve or thirteen. The next available tour is on April 8th. Guests stay at a hotel called The Palace, which is right on the main beach. The basic tour price is $380. All bookings must be made at least four days before travel, and a 20% deposit is required at the time of booking.

mcq.q: What is the minimum age to join North College Library as an external member?
How far in advance can external members book a computer?
How can library items be renewed?
opts: By telephone or the library website

1.errorText: A man calls North College Library to ask about becoming a member. The librarian explains that external members must be over 16. To join, applicants must bring a driving licence plus a credit card showing their address, along with two passport photos. External membership costs £150 per year, or £25 with a valid student card. Members of the public may borrow up to ten items at once. The minimum fine for late returns is £2. Computers can be booked up to 24 hours in advance, and must be booked in blocks of one hour.

; for researchers like me, it remains a place of great intellectual
Antarctica is the highest, coldest and windiest continent and over fifty-eight times the size of the UK.
The ice-cap contains almost 70% of the world's fresh water, but with very low
, most of the continent technically falls into the category of '
Huge icebergs break off the continent each year, and in winter half the surrounding ocean
, which means its size almost doubles.
Research stations are self-contained communities of about twenty people — living space, a kitchen, a small
and a well-equipped gym.
Station Zero One wasn't built on land but on an ice-
. Supplies were brought from a ship fifteen kilometres away at the ice edge.
Snow build-ups buried and finally
four previous stations on the same site — a huge challenge to architects.
The solution: buildings placed on
which can be raised above the changing snow level on legs which are extendable.
Someone in Antarctica needs about 3,500 kilocalories a day — just over
the UK average. This energy comes from foods high in
and fat.
Special ration boxes contain enough food for one person for twenty days, using freeze-
to preserve quality while reducing weight.
My own research was fascinating as a marine
. But climate change research is perhaps the most crucial field of study.
Surveying changes in the ice-cap volume is vital, as these affect world sea levels and ocean
Bubbles in the ice provide an index of
— frozen samples of previous atmospheres over the past 500,000 years.
There are increasing opportunities for
people to work in Antarctica — not only as research assistants but also in administrative and
positions.

signpost.quote: As part of our project, we carried out a survey of people in the street, and the thing that came up over and over again is that people don't think it's easy enough to recycle.
Oddly enough, half the glass that's collected is green, and a lot of that is imported, so more green glass is recycled than the UK needs.
For recycling paper, Britain comes second in Europe with 40%, behind Germany's amazing 70%.
opts: Shifting from glass to a new material

signpost.quote: But these problems have now been solved.
opts: Dismissing past issues and moving to positive developments

signpost.quote: Well, to sum up, there seems to be plenty of research going on into how to re-use materials, but the biggest problem is getting people to think about recycling.
opts: Signalling the end of the presentation with a key conclusion

1.errorText: Jenny Chan is looking for a host family for six months. She would like to live in the south-west of the city. Jenny describes her English as advanced overall. She would prefer a room with a garden view and en suite facilities. She also wants access to a shared computer. She is prepared to pay up to £80 a week, but would go up to £100 if it was something special. She would like to move in on Monday 23rd February.

3.functions: A  Summarising a problem    B  Explaining a root cause    C  Predicting a likely action    D  Expressing optimism    E  Raising a doubt

signpost.quote: So, in this first week, we run a series of talks which focus on different aspects of the library and its resources.
opts: Introducing the programme structure for new students

signpost.quote: Having found what you need, you'll find you can readily save texts on your personal computer space to print off when you need.
opts: Explaining the practical benefit of a resource

signpost.quote: Clearly some of you will find the printed version more accessible as it sits on the shelves, but I'm afraid the intention is to phase these out eventually.
opts: Acknowledging a preference before announcing a change

signpost.quote: As your Training Supervisor, I just oversee your induction and will not be around after this initial week.
opts: Clarifying the speaker's own limited role

signpost.quote: Obviously this is not relevant to those of you who are undergraduates; it is just for postgraduates.
opts: Narrowing the audience for a specific piece of information
